artifacts.summary: W vs D semantic architecture analysis; clarifies that F/P witnesses exist in W (MCSes) but must be indexed by D; countability obstruction is fundamental for D=Int with single history; recommends accepting algebraic/syntax split with conditional completeness for algebraic path

next_steps: Decision needed: (1) Accept sorries as intentional for algebraic path, or (2) Refactor to D=CanonicalMCS. Recommend Option 1 (accept algebraic/syntax split).

key_insights: W (WorldState) and D (Duration) are DISTINCT components in TaskFrame semantics
Histories are functions h: D -> W mapping times to world states
F/P operators quantify over D (times), not W (world states)
Countability obstruction affects what a SINGLE history can cover, not what exists in W
CanonicalFMCS works by making D=W, collapsing the distinction
IntBFMCS sorries are mathematically unfillable due to countability, not proof gaps
